answer:
\boxed{I}  

Quicksort has a worst-case running time of Θ(n²) when the pivot selection is poor, but on average it runs in Θ(n log n). The other listed algorithms either have different worst-case or average-case complexities. For example, Mergesort and Heapsort are Θ(n log n) in both worst and average cases, while Insertion Sort and Bubblesort are Θ(n²) in both.
